title: linum catharticum var. catharticum
intro: Linum catharticum var. catharticum, also known as woolly meadow rue or sea rue, is a perennial plant species native to the Mediterranean region, belonging to the flax family (Linaceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its cathartic properties, meaning it has the ability to stimulate bowel movements and relieve constipation. It is also believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and protect against cell damage. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is often used to support digestive health and alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome.
